question.title: Who will be Iran's Supreme Leader on January 1, 2027?
question.description:
On 28 February 2026, [Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ali_Khamenei) was killed during a series of airstrikes conducted by Israel and the United States. Khamenei had not officially declared a successor prior to his death, though the [New York Times reported](https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/21/world/middleeast/iran-ayatollah-israel-war.html) that he had privately nominated three senior clerics as potential successors: [Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gholam-Hossein_Mohseni-Eje%27i), [Asghar Hijazi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asghar_Hijazi), and [Hassan Khomeini](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hassan_Khomeini).

Under Article 111 of Iran's constitution, a temporary three-member council [was formed](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/1/who-could-succeed-ayatollah-ali-khamenei-to-lead-iran) to handle executive duties until a new Supreme Leader is selected. That council consists of President Masoud Pezeshkian, Supreme Court Chief Justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i, and senior cleric Ayatollah Alireza Arafi, who was appointed from the Guardian Council. Iran's Supreme Leader is formally chosen by the Assembly of Experts, an 88-member clerical body, by simple majority vote. Among [the most frequently discussed candidates](https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/succession-plans-irans-khamenei-hit-top-gear-2025-06-23/) is Hassan Khomeini, the 54-year-old grandson of the Islamic Republic's founder, who is considered a reformist figure. Other contenders include [Mojtaba Khamenei](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mojtaba_Khamenei), the late Supreme Leader's son, who has strong ties to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, though the clerical establishment may resist a father-to-son succession given the regime's revolutionary origins.
question.resolution_criteria:
This question will resove as the individual who is officially the Supreme Leader of Iran on January 1, 2027, at 00:00 Iran Standard Time.

If the position of Supreme Leader is formally abolished or the Islamic Republic of Iran ceases to exist, this question will resolve as **Position abolished**.

If no alive individual is the official Supreme Leader of Iran on January 1, 2027, this question will resolve as **None**.
question.fine_print:
Any interim, acting, or temporary leader will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Only individuals formally appointed to the office titled "Supreme Leader of the Islamic Republic of Iran" (or a direct equivalent replacing that title) will qualify. Individuals serving solely as President, head of state, head of government, or in any other role that is not explicitly the Supreme Leader or a direct constitutional equivalent will not qualify.
